mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, compressing corporate profit margins and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive IC drops below negative 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous surges in industrial production and housing starts indicate broad-based economic acceleration that outpaces monetary accommodation, leading to inflationary pressure and negative equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series rise above their 12-month moving averages, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid rate hikes, rising real short-term rates drain liquidity from equity markets, causing negative forward returns when consumer credit growth simultaneously decelerates.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises greater than 0.25pp in 3 months
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero during identified tightening phases, or the signal predictive power vanishes when controlling for volatility indices.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically translating into positive equity returns over a one-week horizon as valuation multiples stabilize.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions consistently fail to align with subsequent 5-day return directions, or the rolling Spearman correlation drops below 0.10 across multiple decades.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index exceeds 105, manufacturing capacity utilization peaks, signaling impending supply bottlenecks that erode corporate margins and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The index breaches the threshold yet equity returns remain positive, or the threshold signal yields a negative IC over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in employment growth and rising interest rates indicate a liquidity crunch coinciding with weakening labor demand, which severely compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to significantly predict returns when both series move in opposite directions, or the joint signal out-of-sample accuracy falls below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: During high-inflation environments, rising consumer prices erode real disposable income, dampening retail demand and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as earnings estimates are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises greater than 0.3 percent month-over-month
failure_condition: Housing starts remain resilient despite CPI spikes without triggering negative equity returns, or the signal directional accuracy drops below 50 percent in backtests.
